name: Ears
description: Contact microphone with preamp, envelope follower, and gate detector. Mutable's take on Tom Whitwell's open-source Mikrophonie design. Turns physical touches, taps, and external audio into modular CV and gates.

controls.name: GAIN
controls.description: Preamp gain for contact mic or Hi-Z input. Covers line-level boost to tiny contact-mic transients.
controls.detail: 0 to +40dB · CW = more gain · clipping lit by red LED at OUT

controls.name: Contact microphone (piezo disc)
controls.description: Large piezo pad on the front panel. Scratch, tap, rub, brush, or press objects against it for acoustic excitation sources.
controls.detail: Hi-Z jack overrides the mic when a cable is inserted

controls.name: Envelope follower jumpers
controls.description: Three-position jumper for envelope follower attack/release. Third setting (jumper removed) is the longest time.
controls.detail: 3 presets: fast · medium · slow (no jumper = longest)

controls.name: Gate detector jumpers
controls.description: Three-position jumper for the gate threshold. Third setting (jumper removed) is the highest threshold voltage.
controls.detail: 3 presets: low · medium · high (no jumper = highest)

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Hi-Z audio input. Patching here disconnects the contact microphone and routes the external source through the same preamp.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Amplified audio output of the contact mic or external IN. Red LED lights on clipping.

outputs.name: ENV
outputs.description: Envelope follower CV. Tracks the amplitude of the amplified signal. White LED shows CV level.
outputs.voltage: ~0 to +5V · attack/release set by rear jumpers

outputs.name: GATE
outputs.description: Gate output. Goes high whenever the envelope crosses the threshold set by the rear gate-detector jumpers. Orange LED mirrors activity.

behaviors.name: Contact mic normalization
behaviors.description: The piezo contact pad on the panel is normalled into the preamp. Patching any cable into IN disconnects the piezo — useful for using Ears as a clean Hi-Z amp for guitar, bass, or line-level sources without mic crosstalk.

behaviors.name: Envelope follower time jumpers
behaviors.description: Three discrete attack/release presets. Fastest tracks individual transients for percussive triggering; slowest smooths to a pad-like CV. Removing the jumper entirely selects the longest setting — there is no front-panel access, so set once for the session.

behaviors.name: Gate threshold jumpers
behaviors.description: Three gate-detector thresholds. Lowest fires on subtle taps or ambient noise (good for self-triggering). Highest ignores all but hard hits. Removing the jumper gives the highest threshold, useful as a noise gate for loud external signals.

behaviors.name: Envelope + audio simultaneity
behaviors.trigger: GAIN turned up with audio applied
behaviors.description: OUT carries the clean amplified audio while ENV and GATE run in parallel. A single guitar or drum can drive a VCF cutoff (ENV) while being processed as audio (OUT) and triggering an envelope (GATE) at the same time — Ears acts as a three-way gateway from the acoustic world.

behaviors.name: Speaker-feedback chain
behaviors.description: With nothing patched to IN, a monitor or speaker near the Ears panel couples vibration into the piezo. Feeding modular output back into monitors creates a physical acoustic feedback loop controllable via GAIN — Mutable explicitly advertises speaker feedback as a use case.

behaviors.name: Gate from non-percussive audio
behaviors.description: When gate threshold is set to the lowest jumper setting and envelope follower to a slow release, sustained pads or bowed sources produce a hold-high gate that only drops when the source goes silent. Effectively turns Ears into a voice-activated gate for patch routing.

behaviors.name: No S/H or auto-gain
behaviors.description: Despite rumours, Ears has no sample-and-hold mode and no automatic gain control — GAIN is manual, and the envelope/gate path is analog. All adaptive behaviour lives in the jumper presets at the rear of the PCB.

patchIdeas.name: Percussive envelope from taps
patchIdeas.patch: Fast-attack/fast-release envelope jumper · GAIN at noon · tap the piezo pad · ENV → filter cutoff (attenuverter-scaled) · GATE → percussive envelope trigger on a VCA · taps on Ears become plucked notes with dynamic filter sweep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ears:
* Envelope jumper: fast
* Gate jumper: medium
* GAIN: noon

VCO:
Filter:
Envelope:
VCA:

- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
- Ears (ENV) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Ears (GATE) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Voice-controlled vocoder-style filter
patchIdeas.patch: External microphone (or spoken voice picked up by piezo) · slow envelope jumper · GAIN high for sensitivity · ENV → VCF cutoff with attenuverter at 2 o'clock · drone VCO into the filter · voice dynamics open the filter, producing a crude wah/vocoder effect without any digital processing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ears:
* Envelope jumper: slow
* GAIN: ~3 o'clock

VCO:
Filter:

- Microphone (Out) -> Ears (IN)
- VCO (Drone) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)
- Ears (ENV) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Acoustic clock source
patchIdeas.patch: Metronome, kick drum, or clapping hands near the piezo · medium gate threshold · GATE → clock input of a sequencer · Ears converts acoustic rhythm into modular clocks so a live drummer or a metronome app can clock a patch without a MIDI interface.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ears:
* Gate jumper: medium

Sequencer:

- Ears (GATE) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Guitar into modular
patchIdeas.patch: Electric guitar → IN (disconnects piezo) · GAIN at 3 o'clock to hit modular level · OUT → wavefolder → filter → VCA · ENV → wavefolder amount so playing harder pushes into folding · GATE → envelope trigger for note-on dynamics.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ears:
* GAIN: ~3 o'clock

Guitar:
Wavefolder:
Filter:
Envelope:
VCA:

- Guitar (Out) -> Ears (IN)
- Ears (OUT) -> Wavefolder (In) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
- Ears (ENV) >> Wavefolder (Fold CV)
- Ears (GATE) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Speaker-feedback resonator
patchIdeas.patch: Patch a VCO through a high-Q filter into a nearby monitor speaker · speaker vibration couples through case into Ears' piezo · Ears (OUT) → filter FM input · closes an acoustic feedback loop · GAIN knob controls the onset of howl · tapping the case modulates the resonance.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ears:

VCO:
Filter:
Monitor:

- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Monitor (In) // physical speaker near Ears
- Monitor (Acoustic) -> Ears (Piezo)
- Ears (OUT) >> Filter (FM CV) // acoustic feedback loop

patchIdeas.name: Dual-path envelope and audio
patchIdeas.patch: Contact-mic scrape → OUT → granular processor for audio texture · same signal's ENV → granular density CV · GATE → trigger freeze on the granular · single physical gesture drives audio, texture density, and freeze behaviour simultaneously.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ears:
* Envelope jumper: medium
* Gate jumper: high

Granular:

- Ears (OUT) -> Granular (In)
- Ears (ENV) >> Granular (Density CV)
- Ears (GATE) g> Granular (Freeze)
- Granular (Out) -> Output (In)
